name: Amuse

notes: Marriott's current Le Méridien Arlington dining page explicitly says its bar and restaurant are temporarily closed for renovation. No current operating Amuse menu is published.
All 11 frozen rows were reconciled: three are food or drink rows belonging to other Rosslyn businesses, and eight are directory chrome, unrelated business cards, address text, or interview copy.
Marriott publishes no current Amuse allergen matrix, ingredient disclosure, cross-contact disclosure, or operating menu while the restaurant is closed.

The Rosslyn district directory and OpenTable were not promoted to restaurant-issued allergen evidence. The two frozen 'official-ingredients' classifications came from other restaurants on a district-wide feature page and were removed.

The frozen generic-website extraction combined Amuse's Rosslyn identity page with 13 unrelated Rosslyn directory, restaurant, feature, and interview URLs. That cross-location source expansion is the complete cause of all 11 bad rows.

The restaurant-specific repair changes the canonical source to Marriott's current hotel dining page, removes every contaminated row, and quarantines the record until first-party reopening evidence and a current menu exist.

sourceAttempts.outcome: HTTP 200. The current first-party page identifies Amuse but states that the bar and restaurant are temporarily closed for renovation. It publishes no menu or allergen disclosure.
notes: The old hours rendered below the closure banner are treated as stale conflicting fields, not evidence that the restaurant is open.

sourceAttempts.outcome: HTTP 200 error shell saying the page is no longer available. Its embedded Marriott outlet record has menuURL null and provides no current item catalog or allergen disclosure.

The Marriott-linked OpenTable identity was found, but direct artifact capture failed and the public result says the restaurant is not available on OpenTable. Search-rendered breakfast text was not treated as a current operating menu or official allergen evidence.
notes: The official Marriott page's reservation URL identifies OpenTable profile 82636.

sourceAttempts.outcome: Targeted searches for a current Amuse menu, allergen guide, renovation status, and reopening date produced the current Marriott closure page, the retired Marriott restaurant detail, and stale third-party menu text; no current first-party menu or allergen source was found.
The Wayback CDX request timed out after 20 seconds with no response. Historical captures would not override Marriott's current closure notice in any event.
HTTP 200. The Rosslyn district page corroborates Amuse's hotel identity, address, and phone but does not publish a current item catalog or allergen disclosure.
notes: Other Rosslyn pages referenced by the frozen record belong to different businesses and cannot be merged into Amuse.

findings.summary: Every frozen Amuse row came from an unrelated Rosslyn page rather than an Amuse menu.
findings.resolution: Removed all 11 rows, replaced the canonical source with Marriott, and quarantined the temporarily closed restaurant with zero current items.

findings.summary: The apple crisp bowl and Cajun shrimp salad carried official ingredient allergens even though both rows belonged to other Rosslyn businesses.
findings.resolution: Removed both cross-location rows and all associated allergen claims from Amuse.

findings.summary: Marriott's current closure banner conflicts with old hours still rendered lower on the same page and embedded in the retired detail response.
findings.resolution: The explicit current renovation closure notice governs; stale hours and third-party menu text were not used as proof of an operating menu.

repairs.summary: Removed the 11 contaminated rows, switched identity and closure sourcing to Marriott, recorded temporary closure, and quarantined Amuse with zero current items until reopening.

notes: Live non-target restaurant projection SHA-256 remained 55db018c98ec747a158f908e002bed02e0a8d12e5fd895f68bde7bb716cb338c.
A second target-only repair run produced the same target hash and exactly one repair note.
